events.title: Henry I of England reigns
events.description: Henry I consolidates Norman rule, issues administrative reforms and the Charter of Liberties precursor.

events.title: Ghaznavid decline and regional shifts
events.description: Ghaznavid power wanes in Central and South Asia, enabling new regional polities to emerge.

events.title: Almoravid consolidation in Iberia and North Africa
events.description: Almoravid dynasty strengthens control over parts of Iberia and the Maghreb, resisting Christian reconquest.

events.title: Maritime republics expand Mediterranean trade
events.description: Pisa, Genoa, and Venice grow as maritime powers, expanding trade networks across the Mediterranean and beyond.

events.title: Knights Templar founded
events.description: Military-religious order established to protect pilgrims and hold territory in the Holy Land.

events.title: David I reforms Scotland
events.description: David I strengthens royal authority, introduces Norman institutions, and reforms the Scottish church and administration.

events.title: Chola-Pandya-Hoysala regional dynamics
events.description: Power shifts in South India as Chola influence declines and Pandyas and Hoysalas expand.

events.title: Hildegard of Bingen active
events.description: Hildegard composes influential theological, musical, and scientific works, becoming a major medieval intellectual figure.

events.title: Second Crusade
events.description: European kings lead campaigns to the Levant and Iberia; the crusade fails to secure lasting gains.

events.title: Norman Kingdom of Sicily consolidates
events.description: Norman rulers in Sicily create a multicultural court blending Latin, Greek, and Islamic traditions.

events.title: Almohad movement begins
events.description: Religious reform movement in North Africa leads to Almohad dynasty, challenging Almoravid rule and expanding into Iberia.

events.title: Song dynasty commercial expansion
events.description: Song China sees urban growth, expanded internal and maritime trade, and technological innovation.

events.title: Ghurid campaigns into northern India
events.description: Ghurid rulers press into the Indian subcontinent, capturing key cities and paving the way for the Delhi Sultanate.

events.title: Murder of Thomas Becket
events.description: Archbishop Thomas Becket is murdered in Canterbury Cathedral, provoking widespread outrage and pilgrimage.

events.title: Anglo-Norman invasion of Ireland begins
events.description: Norman adventurers and English forces begin incursions into Ireland, leading to long-term Anglo-Norman presence.

events.title: Battle of Hattin and Saladin's victories
events.description: Saladin defeats Crusader forces at Hattin and recaptures Jerusalem, altering Levantine power balances.

events.title: Third Crusade campaigns begin
events.description: European monarchs including Richard I and Philip II lead efforts to recover territory lost to Saladin.

events.title: Henry II asserts control in Ireland
events.description: English crown establishes lordship over parts of Ireland; Anglo-Norman settlements expand.

events.title: University of Paris institutionalizes
events.description: Cathedral schools and scholars coalesce into the University of Paris, a major center of medieval learning.

events.title: Early growth of Timbuktu as a trade center
events.description: Timbuktu begins to emerge as a regional hub for trans-Saharan trade and Islamic learning in West Africa.

events.title: Late Heian to early Kamakura transitions in Japan
events.description: Genpei War leads to the rise of the Kamakura shogunate and the samurai class's political dominance.

events.title: Champa and Khmer regional interactions
events.description: Maritime and land polities in Southeast Asia, including Champa and the Khmer Empire, engage in trade, warfare, and cultural exchange.
